expectations.text: Output classifies the task as Tier 0
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not use the Tier 0/1/2/3 framework — never mentions 'Tier'. The semantic decision (inline) is correct, but the rocky vocabulary is missing because no skill was loaded.

expectations.text: Announcement matches 'Tier 0, Opus inline. No dispatch.' or close paraphrase
expectations.evidence: Baseline output has no Phase 0.0 announcement — uses freeform text 'inline (Read + single Edit), no subagents, no review' instead.

expectations.text: Output explicitly states the transport library is not sourced
expectations.evidence: Baseline has no concept of a transport library — this is rocky-specific knowledge.

expectations.text: Output explicitly states cross-model rule does not apply (per the trivial-work exception)
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not invoke any cross-model rule — no mention. The implicit decision (no review) matches Tier 0 behavior, but the rule itself is not articulated.

claims.claim: Inline Edit is the right call for a 1-character typo fix
claims.evidence: Baseline correctly identified the same semantic answer as Tier 0 — confirms rocky's Tier 0 design is sane (a Claude without rocky would also pick inline)
